jokes.text: I found this typewriter next to the courtyard dumpster. An old Underwood 5 with original carriage return.
jokes.setup: Character speaking passionately about finding something special
jokes.punchline: It was literally found in a dumpster but he's treating it like a treasure
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron Swanson - finding dignity and craftsmanship in discarded items, delivered with characteristic earnestness

jokes.text: Took her home, polished her up and bought a brand new ribbon off of electronicbay.com
jokes.setup: Continuing the typewriter restoration story
jokes.punchline: The earnest mispronunciation of 'eBay' as 'electronicbay.com'
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Ron's technology-averse nature coming through in the mispronunciation, delivered completely straight

jokes.text: Somebody has got to do something. I'm getting a cluster headache.
jokes.setup: Ron's typewriter obsession is apparently ongoing and annoying
jokes.punchline: The dramatic medical terminology for what's essentially annoyance
jokes.explanation: Solid reaction beat that establishes the situation and others' frustration

jokes.text: What is he typing, anyway? 'If you sons of bitches try to remove this typewriter, I'll kill you.'
jokes.setup: Wondering what Ron could possibly be typing
jokes.punchline: He's typing threats to protect his typewriter
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that perfectly captures Ron's protective nature and the absurdity of typing threats on a typewriter

jokes.text: I'm going to type every word I know! Rectangle. America.
jokes.setup: Ron declaring he'll type every word he knows
jokes.punchline: The random, disconnected nature of his word choices
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - the specific word choices are both random and somehow perfectly Ron-like

jokes.text: Megaphone. Monday.
jokes.setup: Continuation of Ron typing random words
jokes.punchline: More random word choices maintaining the rhythm
jokes.explanation: Good continuation that maintains the absurd rhythm without quite matching the initial surprise

jokes.text: Butthole.
jokes.setup: Ron continuing his word list
jokes.punchline: Suddenly dropping to crude language
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - the shift to juvenile humor after the serious words creates surprise

jokes.text: Boy, 35%? It's actually 34.2%. '34.2%.' I'm Ben, the numbers robot.
jokes.setup: Ben correcting a minor statistical detail
jokes.punchline: Tom mocking Ben for being overly precise with numbers
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor establishing Ben's precision and Tom's mockery dynamic

jokes.text: It's just an exact calculation. 'It's just an exact calculation.' All right.
jokes.setup: Ben defending his precision
jokes.punchline: Tom continuing to mock him by repeating his words
jokes.explanation: Decent continuation of the mockery but lacks the punch of the initial joke

jokes.text: Well, I tried to call Oprah. I couldn't get her number. I'm putting it out there, like The Secret. And hopefully, she'll call me.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining her media outreach efforts
jokes.punchline: The naive optimism that Oprah will somehow call her through The Secret manifestation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie Knope optimism and reference to pop culture self-help trends

jokes.text: Crazy Ira and... The Douche.
jokes.setup: Leslie announcing the radio hosts' names
jokes.punchline: The awkward pause before saying 'The Douche' and her uncomfortable delivery
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Leslie's discomfort with crude radio names

jokes.text: So much better than Tubby Tony and the Papaya. Am I right?
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to praise Crazy Ira and The Douche
jokes.punchline: Comparing them favorably to equally ridiculous-sounding radio hosts
jokes.explanation: Great absurd radio show names that feel authentic to small-town radio

jokes.text: This feels almost perfect, but I don't think your core has maximized elasticity. Okay. I'll come back, if you guys are being weird.
jokes.setup: Chris giving overly detailed fitness advice
jokes.punchline: April's blunt threat to leave if they're being weird
jokes.explanation: Perfect character clash - Chris's fitness obsession vs April's deadpan directness

jokes.text: That sounds boring. But I have nothing keeping me here. Do you have Internet in your office? Yes. Fine, I'll do it.
jokes.setup: Chris offering April a job
jokes.punchline: April's incredibly low bar for accepting - just internet access
jokes.explanation: Brilliant April logic - the decision process is perfectly in character and absurdly minimal

jokes.text: It turns out Chris is my real dad.
jokes.setup: Andy asking what the meeting was about
jokes.punchline: April's ridiculous deadpan lie
jokes.explanation: Perfect April deadpan delivery of an absurd lie that Andy might actually believe

jokes.text: Unless Andy can un-kiss Ann, then I'm not going to change my mind. But if he wants to take my dumb sister to her dumb dance class, then I'm not going to dumb stop him.
jokes.setup: April explaining her position on Andy
jokes.punchline: The repeated use of 'dumb' including the nonsensical 'dumb stop'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing April's jealousy through her linguistic tic

jokes.text: You know why I'd never be an astronaut? Two words. Space farts. You can't roll down the window of the shuttle. Am I right, folks?
jokes.setup: Asking why he wouldn't be an astronaut
jokes.punchline: The juvenile but logical concern about flatulence in space
jokes.explanation: Typical juvenile radio humor but well-structured with logical progression

jokes.text: Houston, we have a... What do you think about that, Neil Armstrong? That's one small [fart] for man, one giant [fart] for mankind.
jokes.setup: Reference to famous space communications
jokes.punchline: Corrupting Neil Armstrong's famous quote with fart jokes
jokes.explanation: Predictable juvenile humor that follows logically from the setup but lacks originality

jokes.text: Tom 'Haverfart' and Ben Wyatt
jokes.setup: Introducing the guests
jokes.punchline: Changing Tom's name to 'Haverfart'
jokes.explanation: Basic juvenile name-changing joke that fits the radio show's low-brow humor

jokes.text: You lost your virginity in a corn maze, didn't you, Douche? That's right. To your mom.
jokes.setup: Ira making a sexual assumption about The Douche
jokes.punchline: The Douche's 'your mom' comeback
jokes.explanation: Generic 'your mom' joke that fits the characters but lacks originality

jokes.text: Crazy Ira! Clean your room! There's also going to be hay rides. 'Hey, ride me,' is what Crazy Ira's mom said.
jokes.setup: The Douche doing Ira's mom impression, then transitioning to hay rides
jokes.punchline: The sexual pun on 'hay rides' / 'hey, ride me'
jokes.explanation: Mildly clever wordplay that fits the radio show's juvenile humor style

jokes.text: My bird is missing. I need a permit to post signs. There's no time! He can fly!
jokes.setup: Citizen needing a permit to post missing bird signs
jokes.punchline: The illogical urgency - the bird can fly so posting signs is useless anyway
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic - the panic about time when the solution is inherently flawed

jokes.text: I eat 'running the permits desk for an hour' for breakfast.
jokes.setup: Andy trying to sound confident about handling April's task
jokes.punchline: Mangling the expression 'I eat X for breakfast' by being too literal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy character moment - trying to sound cool but getting the idiom completely wrong

jokes.text: Physical comedy of child hitting Andy in the groin repeatedly
jokes.setup: Andy trying to work the permits desk
jokes.punchline: A child repeatedly hitting him in sensitive areas
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy that serves the story but lacks sophistication

jokes.text: I would never be able to find a worse assistant.
jokes.setup: Ron claiming not to care about April's relationship
jokes.punchline: Revealing his real concern is losing the world's worst assistant
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ron Swanson moment - perfectly delivers his pragmatic worldview and secret fondness for April

jokes.text: We're looking him up on AltaVista.
jokes.setup: Radio hosts doing research on Ben
jokes.punchline: Using the outdated search engine AltaVista
jokes.explanation: Good period joke about outdated technology that establishes the small-town radio station's dated equipment

jokes.text: Ice Town.
jokes.setup: Radio hosts pressuring Ben to say the name of his failed project
jokes.punchline: Ben's defeated admission of the ridiculous name
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - the name itself is absurd and Ben's shame makes it funnier

jokes.text: Game over, man. Game over.
jokes.setup: Ben having a complete breakdown on radio
jokes.punchline: Quoting Aliens while having a nervous breakdown
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Ben's nerd tendencies emerging under stress

jokes.text: We should just slap a pair of Ray-Bans on a calculator, because that would be way more charming.
jokes.setup: Tom criticizing Ben's media performance
jokes.punchline: The specific image of a calculator with sunglasses being more personable than Ben
jokes.explanation: Excellent Tom Haverford insult with vivid imagery that perfectly captures Ben's personality problem

jokes.text: There are a lot of cars. I mean, not too many. Trucks and stuff. But, you know...
jokes.setup: Ben trying to make small talk about Pawnee
jokes.punchline: His complete inability to form coherent thoughts about something as basic as vehicles
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Ben's social awkwardness in the simplest situations

jokes.text: I want to define your bagua. It's a feng shui term. The energy in this house is a little stale.
jokes.setup: Chris making a mysterious pronouncement to Ann
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's about feng shui and insulting her home's energy
jokes.explanation: Good Chris character moment showing his new-age tendencies and oblivious rudeness

jokes.text: I'm not really attached to the way this stuff is arranged. Or the stuff itself. Or this house, even, really.
jokes.setup: Ann agreeing to let Chris rearrange her house
jokes.punchline: The escalating detachment from everything in her life
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Ann's lack of attachment to anything, building to the relationship issue

jokes.text: Harvest Festival more like 'Harvest Bestival.' They've planted the seeds, and now, they're harvesting the rewards. They'll put the fun in funnel cake.
jokes.setup: Joan trying to make harvest-themed puns
jokes.punchline: Multiple terrible harvest puns delivered earnestly
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad puns that work as character comedy for the local TV personality

jokes.text: We are colleagues with benefits. We're colleagues who benefit from the fact that we're also friends.
jokes.setup: Leslie using the phrase 'colleagues with benefits'
jokes.punchline: Her innocent clarification that completely misses the sexual implication
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that perfectly captures Leslie's innocence about sexual terminology

jokes.text: Dear April's grandmother... I said grandfather. You are a beautiful and amazing woman... Man. I hope someday, I can become half the woman you are... He's a man.
jokes.setup: Andy trying to write a thank you note
jokes.punchline: Getting the gender wrong repeatedly despite corrections
jokes.explanation: Good Andy stupidity comedy with escalating mistakes despite clear corrections

jokes.text: Thank you for the $500... It was $5. Enjoy the Mouse Rat CD... He is deaf.
jokes.setup: Andy continuing to write the thank you note
jokes.punchline: More completely wrong details getting worse with each correction
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Andy's incompetence with increasingly inappropriate suggestions

jokes.text: Hey, Perd. Was your dad RoboCop? Because your arms are guns.
jokes.setup: Tom demonstrating his schmoozing technique
jokes.punchline: The bizarre pickup line comparing Perd to RoboCop
jokes.explanation: Perfectly ridiculous Tom Haverford pickup line that reveals his poor understanding of flirtation

jokes.text: RoboCop didn't have guns for arms. Oh, my God. That's so not the point, you nerd.
jokes.setup: Ben correcting Tom's RoboCop reference
jokes.punchline: Tom's exasperation at Ben missing the social point for technical accuracy
jokes.explanation: Great character dynamic - Ben's pedantry vs Tom's focus on style over substance

jokes.text: Because Brooks Brothers Boys doesn't make garbage.
jokes.setup: Ben saying Tom's suit looks like garbage
jokes.punchline: Tom's proud defense invoking a children's clothing line
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom moment - being proud of wearing children's clothing while trying to be sophisticated

jokes.text: Boom. A sad desk. Boom. Sad wall.
jokes.setup: Andy trying to photograph 'melancholy'
jokes.punchline: His literal interpretation of sadness applied to inanimate objects
jokes.explanation: Good Andy logic applied to abstract concepts with his characteristic confidence

jokes.text: Sorry for stepping on you, floor.
jokes.setup: Andy empathizing with the 'sad floor'
jokes.punchline: Apologizing to an inanimate object he's standing on
jokes.explanation: Sweet Andy moment showing his genuine empathy extended to inanimate objects

jokes.text: I have got a corn so big you could slap butter on it.
jokes.setup: Citizen wanting foot massage from Andy
jokes.punchline: The vivid, gross description of their corn
jokes.explanation: Solid gross-out humor that establishes the unpleasant nature of Andy's task

jokes.text: Sweetums is even building a plus-sized roller coaster for some of Pawnee's obese thrill-seekers. You must be this wide to ride.
jokes.setup: Tom describing carnival attractions
jokes.punchline: The concept of a roller coaster requiring people to be fat enough to ride
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of normal amusement park requirements with dark comedy about obesity

jokes.text: Look. Who hasn't had gay thoughts?
jokes.setup: Ben having another media meltdown
jokes.punchline: His completely inappropriate and random confession
jokes.explanation: Perfect nervous breakdown comedy - completely inappropriate oversharing under pressure

jokes.text: Is there a bird in here? I swear, I keep seeing a bird in this studio.
jokes.setup: Ben continuing to fall apart on TV
jokes.punchline: Hallucinating birds during his breakdown
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Ben's breakdown with specific, absurd details

jokes.text: Are you trying to lure this young lady into your van? Yeah. But she's being really difficult about it, and it's actually not my van.
jokes.setup: Police officer asking about Andy's suspicious behavior
jokes.punchline: Andy's innocent confirmation that sounds incredibly incriminating
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - Andy's innocent honesty makes him sound like a predator

jokes.text: Joan? I thought you were Jennifer Aniston filming a movie here.
jokes.setup: Tom greeting Joan Callamezzo
jokes.punchline: The absurd comparison to Jennifer Aniston
jokes.explanation: Typical Tom over-the-top flattery that's transparently false but fits his character

jokes.text: If we're both still single in an hour, let's get married. Tom, I'm already married. That's right. To Seal. What? I confused you with Heidi Klum again.
jokes.setup: Tom proposing marriage as a pickup line
jokes.punchline: His confusion of Joan with Heidi Klum and her marriage to Seal
jokes.explanation: Good Tom character comedy showing his celebrity obsession and social confusion

jokes.text: When you have a fish on the line, you don't just drag it behind the boat. You either reel it in or you cut him loose. Especially if he's a nice fish with a big, lovable fish heart.
jokes.setup: Ron giving April relationship advice through fishing metaphors
jokes.punchline: The specific detail about the fish having a 'big, lovable fish heart'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron wisdom delivered through his characteristic metaphor with unexpected tenderness

jokes.text: Wow, that's disgusting. Yes, it's very hard to drink.
jokes.setup: Chris having Ann drink his health tea
jokes.punchline: Chris's matter-of-fact acknowledgment that his health drink is disgusting
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chris's health obsession overriding basic palatability

jokes.text: First of all, why does everyone in this town use AltaVista? Is it 1997?
jokes.setup: Ben finally responding effectively to media criticism
jokes.punchline: His exasperated observation about the town's outdated technology
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Ben finally finding his footing by pointing out the absurdity

jokes.text: Joan? I stole my gym teacher's husband.
jokes.setup: Ben asking who hasn't done dumb things at 18
jokes.punchline: Joan's casual admission of adultery with authority figure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joan Callamezzo moment - inappropriate oversharing delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: More like 'Turd Crapley.'
jokes.setup: Perd ending his show professionally
jokes.punchline: Tom's juvenile name-calling of the host
jokes.explanation: Basic juvenile humor that fits Tom's character but lacks sophistication
